title: Dizziness in GP

presenting_history.text: Specifies symptom description, timing, triggers, concurrent symptoms, age, and pre-existing conditions.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact DHA history section supports symptom description, timing, triggers, concurrent symptoms, age, conditions, functional impact, and duration without assigning a dizziness syndrome.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Specifies symptom description, timing, triggers, concurrent symptoms, age, and pre-existing conditions.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dizziness

red_flags.text: Lists cardiac, neurologic, trauma, intoxication, visual, consciousness, and severe-headache concerns.
red_flags.rationale: The exact red-flag section supports clinician-assessed cardiac, neurological, trauma, intoxication, visual, consciousness, and severe-headache concerns plus available examination without asserting absence.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists cardiac, neurologic, trauma, intoxication, visual, consciousness, and severe-headache concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dizziness

red_flags.text: Identifies emergency and specialist referral features for dizziness.
red_flags.rationale: The exact referral section supports clinician-entered safety-net, referral status, and follow-up without assigning urgency automatically.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Identifies emergency and specialist referral features for dizziness.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dizziness

red_flags.text: Supports focal neurological and hearing red flags, acute vestibular syndrome, HINTS by trained clinicians, neuroimaging referral, and Hallpike assessment for positional vertigo.
red_flags.rationale: The exact adult neurological section was reviewed for focal-neurological, hearing, acute vestibular, positional, trained-exam, imaging, and referral qualifiers; no specific unconfirmed finding was mapped.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports focal neurological and hearing red flags, acute vestibular syndrome, HINTS by trained clinicians, neuroimaging referral, and Hallpike assessment for positional vertigo.

source.title: Suspected neurological conditions: recognition and referral

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.7 — dizziness and vertigo in adults

examination.text: States that tests depend on history and examination and should be documented only when clinically deemed necessary.
examination.rationale: The exact investigation section supports recording tests already reviewed when clinically selected from history and examination; it does not generate a test.
examination.evidence_extract: States that tests depend on history and examination and should be documented only when clinically deemed necessary.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dizziness

source_limitations: Exact syndrome, observations, orthostatic measurements, hearing, gait, neurological examination, cause, diagnosis, tests, medicine, referral, urgency, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
